data.title: Market Chatter: Takeda Pharmaceutical to Invest $30 Billion in US Over Five Years to Strengthen R&D, Manufacturing
data.description:
Takeda Pharmaceutical will invest $30 billion in the US over the next five years to upgrade manufacturing sites and boost research and development, CEO Christophe Weber said during an earnings call Thursday, Nikkei Asia reported Friday.

Weber said the spending will ensure Takeda's facilities are as efficient as possible and help maintain its US presence, the report said.

Takeda currently runs seven US manufacturing sites, including in Minnesota and California, producing drugs like Entyvio and plasma-derived therapies, the report added.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Mediobanca suitor Monte dei Paschi beats forecasts with 24% profit rise
data.description:
Key points:
  • MPS, Mediobanca both post higher than expected earnings
  • MPS says Banca Generali deal does not undermine its project
  • Surging net fees, lower costs and loan loss charges drive profit

State-backed Monte dei Paschi di Siena on Friday posted a larger-than-forecast 24% quarterly profit rise as it pursues a hostile takeover of rival Mediobanca in the consolidation wave sweeping Italian finance.

Mediobanca, which has announced its own buyout offer for wealth manager Banca Generali in an effort to thwart Monte dei Paschi's (MPS) bid, also reported better than expected results on Friday.

MPS said that work on the Mediobanca bid was progressing and it was not threatened by the planned Banca Generali acquisition.

By contrast, Mediobanca said the risks of combining with MPS were further heightened by the Banca Generali tie-up.

After a deep bad loan cleanup and record profits driven by higher interest rates, Italian banks have turned to dealmaking to support shareholder returns now that rates are falling.

MPS, which Italy has been successfully re-privatising since late 2023 after rescuing it in 2017, is proposing plugging Mediobanca's wealth management and investment banking businesses into its own commercial network, where the two lenders already partner in consumer finance.

Mediobanca, a former powerhouse of Italian capitalism as it started as a lender to large companies, wants to bet instead on wealth management for top tier clients with the Banca Generali acquisition, and continue to steer away from commercial banking.

Mediobanca shares rose fractionally at open, while MPS gained 1.3%. Italy's banking index (.FTITLMS3010) gained 0.5%.

MPS reported net profit of 413 million euros ($464 million), well above a 341 million euro forecast in an analyst consensus gathered by the bank, as revenues held up and costs and loan loss provisions declined.

Revenues were flat year-on-year at 1 billion euros, topping expectations, with higher net fees offseting an 8% decline in income from the gap between lending and deposit rates. Fees from wealth management and advisory in particular shot up 21% on a quarterly basis.

The core capital ratio strengthened further to 19.6% of assets, one of the highest in the sector.

($1 = 0.8908 euros)

data.title: South Korean Stocks Log Fourth Weekly Win
data.description:
The benchmark KOSPI edged lower to close at 2,577 points on Friday, as losses in battery makers offset strong gains among auto stocks.

However, the index still rose 0.7% for the week, marking a fourth straight weekly advance, supported by optimism over U.S. trade talks with major economies.

President Trump and UK Prime Minister Starmer unveiled a limited trade deal that keeps the 10% U.S. tariff on British exports but eases duties on cars and expands agricultural access.

Meanwhile, China expressed confidence in managing trade tensions with the U.S. ahead of key tariff talks set for this weekend in Switzerland.

South Korea is also set to hold follow-up talks with the U.S. next week, as both sides work toward finalizing a trade package by July.

Among battery stocks, LG Energy Solutions and Samsung SDI shed 2.9% and 1.1%, respectively.

Additionally, shipbuilders and biopharmaceutical firms faced pressure.

On the upside, automakers Hyundai Motor and Kia Corp both climbed over 1.5%.

data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at 1:05 p.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was down 1.% at 79,533, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 declined 1.07% to 24,013, on course for second straight session of losses after Pakistan armed forces launched multiple overnight attacks along India's western border.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee rose 0.2% versus the U.S. dollar to 85.5175, as RBI likely intervened to support rupee amid pressure due to intensifying India-Pakistan conflict, traders said.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN067934G=CC) was quoted at 102.62 rupees, with the yield up 2 bps at 6.4133%, as traders nervously trimmed positions after Pakistani forces launched multiple attacks along India’s western border.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was down 2 basis points at 5.64%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) fell nearly 2 basis points to 5.67%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 5.60%, and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 5.90%

data.title: Silver Holds Steady Ahead of Key US-China Trade Talks
data.description:
Silver prices steadied around $32.50 per ounce on Friday, following a volatile week as investors adopted a cautious stance ahead of high-stakes US-China trade negotiations scheduled for this weekend in Switzerland.

The upcoming talks will see Chinese Vice Premier He Lifeng meet with US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent , as both sides seek to ease escalating tariff tensions.

The market is closely watching for any signals of de-escalation that could impact the broader commodity landscape.

Meanwhile, US President Donald Trump announced a preliminary trade deal with the UK—the first since the imposition of sweeping tariffs last month.

Elsewhere, US Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell reinforced a cautious monetary policy stance, warning of mounting risks to inflation and employment while dismissing calls for a preemptive rate cut to offset the economic fallout from tariffs.

data.title: Sweco to Buy Dutch Civil Engineering Consultancy Juust
data.description:
Sweco (SWEC-B.ST, SWEC-A.ST) agreed to buy Dutch civil engineering and urban planning consultancy Juust as it looks to grow the business.

The Swedish engineering consultancy said Friday that Juust will be immediately consolidated into the company. Juust's 2024 turnover totaled 4.1 million euros.

data.title: Bpost jumps on EBIT beat, confirmed outlook despite strike impact in Q1
data.description:
** Shares of Bpost are up 10% after the struggling Belgian postal operator beat Q1 expectations and reiterated its FY adj. EBIT outlook

** Q1 adj. EBIT was EUR 41.6 million euros ($46.77 million), down more than 40% year-on-year, but still ahead of the EUR 34.2 million estimated in the company-provided consensus

** "Despite being a weak quarter, 1Q25 results came in ahead of expectations," analysts at KBC say in a note

** The result includes EUR 6 million impact on operating income level from a two-week strike in February

** KBC analysts say they are pleased to see Bpost reiterating its outlook, considering it now includes the strike impact

** The shares are on track for their best day since September 2023 if the gains hold

($1 = 0.8891 euros)

($1 = 0.8894 euros)

data.title: The DAX Index Opens 0.79% Higher
data.description:
In Frankfurt, the DAX Index is gaining 185 points or 0.79 percent on Friday.

Leading the gains are Siemens Energy (3.22%), Daimler Truck Holding AG (2.46%) and Bayer (2.35%).

Top losers are Puma (-1.71%), Zalando (-0.95%) and Henkel (-0.53%).

data.title: European Stocks Edge Cautiously Higher
data.description:
Both the STOXX 50 and the STOXX 600 rose 0.3% on Friday, extending gains from the previous session, as traders remained cautiously optimistic about the US-UK trade deal and looked ahead to US-China trade negotiations over the weekend.

Oil and gas, autos, and healthcare were the best-performing sectors, while insurance lagged behind.

Meanwhile, investors also monitored corporate earnings.

Shares of Commerzbank edged up just 0.3%, despite the lender reporting a better-than-expected net profit in Q1.

Mediobanca shares gained 1.5% following an earnings beat.

For the week, both benchmark indexes saw only modest moves, with the STOXX 50 up 0.3% and the STOXX 600 slipping 0.1%.

data.title: Iran foreign min to visit Saudi Arabia, Qatar on Saturday, spokesperson says
data.description:
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi will visit Saudi Arabia and Qatar on Saturday and will participate in the Iran-Arab World Dialogue summit in Doha, a ministry spokesperson said on Friday, ahead of possible U.S.-Iran talks on Sunday.

A fourth round of nuclear talks between Iran and the United States is likely to take place over the weekend in the capital of Oman, and U.S. President Donald Trump is set to visit Saudi Arabia, Qatar and the United Arab Emirates next week.

Trump, who withdrew the U.S. from a 2015 nuclear deal between Tehran and world powers, has threatened to bomb Iran if no agreement is reached with his administration to resolve the long-standing dispute.

data.title: Serb-Montenegrin tie-up best bidder for Podgorica-Danilovgrad expressway design
data.description:
PODGORICA (Montenegro), May 9 (SeeNews) - Montenegro's state-run motorway operator Monteput said it has selected a joint offer by Belgrade-based Louis Berger and Podgorica-based Decom Group as the best one in a tender for a conceptual design of a planned expressway that will connect the capital Podgorica to the central town of Danilovgrad.

Niksic-based firm Geostandard will act as a subcontractor, Monteput said in a statement on Thursday, without specifying if any other bids had been submitted for the job.

The agreed value of the contract is 55,200 euro ($62,400) without VAT.

Under the tender terms, the contractor is required to draw the design within four months after the contract signing. The study will determine the optimal route and all technical parameters for the project.

Via Danilovgrad, the planned expressway will also connect Podgorica to Niksic, Montenegro's second largest city. The existing Podgorica-Danilovgrad road cannot serve as a high-speed road because it has many intersections and a large number of junctions on both sides requiring speed limits at many points, Monteput has said.

Danilovgrad's current urban and spatial planning envisages two different routes for the planned expressway - one of 11 km and another one of 18 km, depending on where the expressway will connect to the future Bar-Boljare motorway that will link the country's Adriatic coast with the northern border with Serbia.

($ = 0.885 euro)

data.title: Bavarian Nordic tops STOXX after Q1 revenue beat
data.description:
** Shares in Bavarian Nordic rise around 12% to their highest since end February after the Danish biotech's Q1 revenue came in above market expectations

** Its Q1 revenue was DKK 1,347 million ($203.08 million) against the company-compiled consensus estimate of DKK 995 million

** Kempen says the revenue was boosted by few larger existing public preparedness orders recorded in the first quarter

** If gains hold, the stock will see its biggest one-day rise since August 2024; it is at the top of pan-European STOXX 600 index

($1 = 6.6327 Danish crowns)
